Source_Species.reason: The study used Arabidopsis plants and Arabidopsis cDNA to analyze thiamin-pathway genes, including TH2 expression.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results state downstream of TMP was not engineered; Conclusions state TH2 implementation would not benefit biofortification approaches.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Conclusions explicitly say TH2 is not limiting and biofortification approaches would not benefit from implementing it, supporting low-priority engineering value.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Figures 3-4 measure combined TPK1/TPK2 expression by RT-qPCR, and TPP/total B1 were measured by LC-MS/MS.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Results show THIC overexpression did not notably increase TPP nor TPK expression; multigene lines showed some TPP increase without TPK engineering; Discussion/Conclusions state endogenous TPK is sufficient and not limiting.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: TPK expression was measured in 15-d-old complete seedlings grown in vitro on half-strength MS plus sucrose.

Omics_Data.reason: Targeted LC-MS/MS and RT-qPCR are explicit; no accession numbers are reported in the excerpt.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Conclusions say biofortification approaches would not benefit from implementing TPK; Discussion also cites rice data where TPK overexpression did not raise B1.

Source_Species.reason: Arabidopsis cDNA was used to amplify THIC CDS.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods specify transformed plants were Arabidopsis Columbia-0/Col-0.

Applied_Species.reason: All overexpression experiments were done in Arabidopsis.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Abstract and Figure 2 legend explicitly describe THIC as HMP-P synthase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: THIC is directly described as the thiamin biosynthesis HMP-P synthase enzyme.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: THIC is one of the three biosynthesis genes responsible for B1/thiamin synthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper explicitly states THIC executes synthesis of the pyrimidine moiety/branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: THIC is presented as an upstream branch synthesis step and a control point in the pathway.

Primary_Product.reason: Results link THIC overexpression to accumulation of HMP(-P)/total HMP, consistent with HMP-P as its direct product leading to total HMP pool.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper explicitly states THIC synthesizes the pyrimidine moiety HMP-P for thiamin biosynthesis.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: THIC-derived pyrimidine is explicitly discussed as precursor supply for B1 vitamers.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Discussion states THIC is a control point in thiamin biosynthesis, but single-gene overexpression had limited effect on B1/TPP, so alone it is insufficient for strong B1 increase.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: THIC was constitutively overexpressed and evaluated by RT-qPCR plus LC-MS/MS in single- and multigene lines.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed points are supported: THIC overexpression raised THIC transcript and total HMP up to ~40-fold; limited effect on thiamin/TPP; higher transgenic THIC often coincided with thiamin in multigene lines; abnormal growth on soil and need for balancing/detoxification are discussed.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results and Discussion support massive pyrimidine accumulation with only limited B1 increase unless combined with other steps.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The study connects THIC manipulation to thiamin and total B1 vitamers.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states vitamin B1/TPP is essential for carbon metabolism and human nutrition.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Metabolite quantification was performed on 15-d-old complete seedlings.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper explicitly states endogenous THIC is under riboswitch feedback and circadian promoter control.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: THIC was overexpressed and analyzed in 15-d-old complete seedlings.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Seedling growth conditions, sucrose concentration, mid-light sampling, and abnormal phenotype on soil are explicitly described.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Constructs used the 35S promoter; Figure 2 and text explicitly state strong constitutive expression driven by the 35S promoter.

Omics_Data.reason: RT-qPCR and targeted LC-MS/MS were used; no accession number is given in the excerpt, only mention of Supplemental Table S1.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Discussion states abnormal soil growth and likely toxicity from pyrimidine breakdown products associated with excess pyrimidine accumulation.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly discusses the THIC 3'UTR riboswitch and circadian regulation, rationale for engineering THIC, and need for balancing/salvage/detoxification.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports THIC as an important target but indicates it should not be overexpressed alone; balancing with thiazole branch and salvage is recommended.

Source_Species.reason: Arabidopsis cDNA was used to amplify THI1 CDS.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Abstract and Figure 2 legend identify THI1 as HET-P synthase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper identifies THI1 as the thiamin thiazole-biosynthesis enzyme and discusses its suicidal nature.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: THI1 is one of the three B1 biosynthesis genes studied.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper explicitly states THI1 executes the thiazole branch/moiety synthesis.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: THI1 is an upstream branch enzyme and Discussion identifies it as a rate-limiting control point.

Primary_Product.reason: Results directly connect THI1 expression with accumulation of HET(-P)/total HET, consistent with HET-P as primary product.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper explicitly states THI1 synthesizes the thiazole moiety HET-P for thiamin biosynthesis.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: THI1-generated thiazole intermediate is explicitly linked to downstream B1 vitamer accumulation.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Discussion explicitly states THI1 activity controls flux through B1 biosynthesis and acts as a rate-limiting control point under these conditions.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: THI1 was constitutively overexpressed and examined by RT-qPCR and LC-MS/MS in single- and multigene lines.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed findings are supported by Results/Discussion: increased THI1 transcript and thiazole intermediates, strongest single-gene increase in B1 vitamers, identification as rate-limiting control point, salvage insufficiency inferred from HET accumulation, and energetic burden from suicidal enzyme nature.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Supported by Results and Discussion: THI1 most effectively boosts B1 vitamers among single-gene interventions and is key to pathway flux.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: THI1 manipulation is linked to increased TMP, thiamin, TPP, and total B1.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states B1/TPP is essential for carbon metabolism and human nutrition.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Discussion states THI1 transcript abundance is very high in WT Arabidopsis relative to other thiamin genes; seedlings are the tissue examined.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: THI1 was overexpressed and analyzed in 15-d-old complete seedlings.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Growth on half-strength MS with 10 g/L sucrose and mid-light sampling are explicitly given.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Constructs used the CaMV 35S promoter for constitutive expression.

Omics_Data.reason: RT-qPCR and targeted LC-MS/MS are explicit; no accession numbers are reported in the excerpt.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Discussion explicitly describes THI1 as energetically expensive/suicidal and notes accumulation of nonphosphorylated HET due to insufficient salvage.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly proposes nonsuicidal THI1 variants and improved salvage/transport as future engineering opportunities.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper strongly supports THI1 as a major engineering target and suggests stacking with other steps/salvage for best B1 enhancement.

Source_Species.reason: Arabidopsis cDNA was used to amplify TH1 CDS.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species is explicitly Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species.reason: All overexpression experiments were performed in Arabidopsis.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Abstract and Figure 2 legend explicitly call TH1 the bifunctional HMP-P kinase/TMP pyrophosphorylase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: TH1 is explicitly described as a bifunctional thiamin biosynthesis enzyme.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: TH1 is one of the three B1 biosynthesis genes engineered.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper states TH1 condenses thiazole and pyrimidine entities to form TMP, linking the two branches.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: TH1 is presented as the midstream condensation step after branch formation.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper clearly says TH1 condenses pyrimidine and thiazole intermediates and performs HMP-P kinase/TMP pyrophosphorylase functions, but the exact substrate notation 'HMP-P/HMP-PP and HET-P' is not explicitly written in this excerpt.

Primary_Product.reason: TH1 forms TMP; this is explicitly stated.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The description matches the explicitly stated TH1 functions: HMP-P kinase/TMP pyrophosphorylase and condensation to TMP.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: TMP is explicitly the first B1 vitamer formed in de novo biosynthesis and immediate product of TH1 activity.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Discussion states TH1 activity is not limiting alone, but extra TH1 aids thiamin accumulation when THIC and THI1 are stimulated.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: TH1 was constitutively overexpressed and tested by RT-qPCR plus LC-MS/MS, including comparison of TH1 single-gene with TT and TTT lines.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed findings are supported: strong TH1 transcript increase, limited overall B1 effect in single lines, strongest effect on TMP, not limiting alone, and beneficial in TTT versus TT backgrounds.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results/Discussion support that TH1 alone mainly increases TMP modestly, but boosts thiamin when combined with THIC and THI1.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: TH1 engineering is discussed in relation to thiamin and total B1 vitamers.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states vitamin B1/TPP supports essential carbon metabolism and human nutrition.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Metabolites were measured in 15-d-old complete seedlings.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Discussion explicitly says TH1 transcript abundance in WT Arabidopsis is roughly 100-fold lower than THIC, TH2, and TPK and almost 7,000-fold lower than THI1.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: TH1 was overexpressed and analyzed in 15-d-old complete seedlings.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Growth medium, sucrose concentration, seedling age, and mid-light sampling are explicitly reported.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Constructs were driven by the 35S promoter.

Omics_Data.reason: RT-qPCR and targeted LC-MS/MS are explicit; no accession IDs are reported in the excerpt.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly notes very low natural TH1 expression and suggests extra TH1 becomes beneficial when precursor supply is boosted; increased nonphosphorylated intermediates may reflect breakdown rather than productive flux.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The study supports TH1 as a useful stacking/supportive target rather than a strong standalone target.
